proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have different mean pfs_months than male patients in the overall cohort.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.

analyses.result_summary: OLS PFS on age_years: beta=0.1765 mo per year, p=0. Direction is OPPOSITE to the conventional clinical expectation: older patients have LONGER PFS in this cohort.
OLS: beta=-0.0060 mo, p=0.77. No detectable sex difference in PFS.
OLS: per-unit ECOG beta=-1.1897 mo, p=0. Mean PFS by ECOG 0/1/2 = 5.27 / 4.05 / 2.91 mo. Strong, monotonic detrimental effect.
OLS: beta=-1.3520 mo, p=0. Stage IV reduces PFS by ~1.35 mo on average.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.

analyses.result_summary: OLS: cea_ng_ml beta=-0.00535 mo per ng/mL, p=3.13e-17. Direction is detrimental as expected.
OLS: albumin_g_dl beta=0.4654, p=3.25e-115. Direction is favorable as expected.
OLS: ldh_u_l beta=-0.00032, p=0.0126. Small detrimental effect.
OLS: weight_loss_pct_6mo beta=-0.0730, p=2.21e-167. Strongly detrimental.
OLS: crp_mg_l beta=-0.00202, p=0.065. Trend toward detrimental but not at p<0.05.
OLS: nlr beta=0.00522, p=0.29. Not significant.
OLS: hemoglobin_g_dl beta=-0.01009, p=0.0777. Not significant; sign even slightly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Right-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided in the overall cohort.
KRAS-mutated tumors (kras_mutation=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months than KRAS wild-type in the overall cohort.
NRAS-mutated tumors (nras_mutation=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months than NRAS wild-type in the overall cohort.
BRAF V600E-mutant tumors (braf_v600e=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months than BRAF wild-type in the overall cohort.
MSI-high tumors (msi_high=1) have different mean pfs_months than MSS tumors in the overall cohort.
HER2-amplified tumors (her2_amplified=1) have different mean pfs_months than non-amplified in the overall cohort.
NTRK-fusion-positive tumors (ntrk_fusion=1) have different mean pfs_months than NTRK-negative in the overall cohort.

analyses.result_summary: PFS: 4.42 (left) vs 4.11 (right); beta=-0.3052, p=5.23e-46. Right-sided is detrimental as expected.
PFS: 4.45 (wt) vs 4.12 (mut); beta=-0.3268, p=8.53e-56. KRAS mutation detrimental.
PFS: 4.30 (wt) vs 4.53 (mut); beta=0.2211, p=0.000228. Surprising: NRAS-mutant patients have a small but significant LONGER PFS overall.
PFS: 4.32 (wt) vs 4.09 (mut); beta=-0.2279, p=3.69e-06. BRAF V600E detrimental as expected.
PFS: 4.31 (MSS) vs 4.29 (MSI-H); beta=-0.0192, p=0.682. No detectable PFS difference by MSI status overall.
beta=-0.0556, p=0.354. No detectable HER2 effect on PFS overall.
beta=0.0399, p=0.783. No detectable NTRK-fusion effect on PFS overall (n=251 fusion-positive).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have LONGER mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: 4.32 (off) vs 4.29 (on); beta=-0.0380, p=0.0894. No significant marginal benefit.
beta=-0.0191, p=0.354. No marginal effect.
beta=0.0052, p=0.855. No marginal effect.
beta=0.0054, p=0.873. No marginal effect.
beta=-0.0436, p=0.246. No marginal effect.

Mean PFS: 4.12 (off) vs 5.09 (on); beta=0.9722 mo, p=0. Strong marginal PFS benefit. Surprising magnitude — far larger than the regorafenib effect reported in real CRC trials. Worth investigating heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months: cetuximab benefit is larger in KRAS wild-type than in KRAS mutant patients.
Within KRAS wild-type patients (kras_mutation=0), treatment_cetuximab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
Within KRAS mutant patients (kras_mutation=1), treatment_cetuximab has no effect or a detrimental effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient beta=0.0196, p=0.665. No significant interaction. Contrary to the strong real-world expectation.
In KRAS wt (n=29102): beta=-0.0475, p=0.124. Refuted: no significant cetuximab benefit even in KRAS wt.
In KRAS mut (n=20898): beta=-0.0279, p=0.376. Effect indistinguishable from zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × nras_mutation interaction on pfs_months: cetuximab benefit is greater in NRAS wild-type.
There is a treatment_cetuximab × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months: cetuximab benefit is greater in BRAF V600E wild-type.
There is a treatment_cetuximab × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months: cetuximab benefit is greater for left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.0067, p=0.96. No interaction.
Interaction beta=-0.0263, p=0.805. No interaction.
Interaction beta=0.0256, p=0.583. No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the canonical cetuximab-eligible subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 [left-sided]), treatment_cetuximab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
In pan-RAS/BRAF wild-type, RIGHT-sided patients (kras=0, nras=0, braf=0, right_sided_primary=1), treatment_cetuximab has no benefit on pfs_months (a contrast subgroup to h7.1).
In any-RAS/BRAF mutant patients, treatment_cetuximab is associated with no benefit (or harm) on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_cetuximab', df.query('kras_mutation==0 and nras_mutation==0 and braf_v600e==0 and right_sided_primary==0')).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In pan-wt left-sided (n=16443, treated=5067): beta=-0.0667, p=0.129. Hypothesis REFUTED — no significant cetuximab benefit even in canonical eligible subgroup.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_cetuximab', df.query('kras_mutation==0 and nras_mutation==0 and braf_v600e==0 and right_sided_primary==1')).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In pan-wt right-sided (n=8881): beta=-0.0262, p=0.587. Null.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_cetuximab', df.query('kras_mutation==1 or nras_mutation==1 or braf_v600e==1')).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In any KRAS/NRAS/BRAF mutant (n=24676): beta=-0.0337, p=0.252. Null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months: pembrolizumab benefit is larger in MSI-high than MSS patients.
Within MSI-high patients (msi_high=1), treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
Within MSS patients (msi_high=0), treatment_pembrolizumab has no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=0.0020, p=0.988. No interaction. Contrary to clinical expectation.
In MSI-H (n=2513): beta=0.0071, p=0.955. REFUTED.
In MSS (n=47487): beta=0.0051, p=0.861. Null as expected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months: encorafenib benefit is concentrated in BRAF V600E mutant patients.
Within BRAF V600E patients (braf_v600e=1), treatment_encorafenib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
Within BRAF wild-type patients (braf_v600e=0), treatment_encorafenib has no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.1413, p=0.376. No interaction. Direction of point estimate is opposite to clinical expectation.
In BRAF V600E (n=2272): beta=-0.1288, p=0.359. Hypothesis REFUTED.
In BRAF wt (n=47728): beta=0.0125, p=0.719. Null as expected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months: benefit is concentrated in HER2-amplified tumors.
Within HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified=1), treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
Within HER2 non-amplified patients (her2_amplified=0), treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=0.0625, p=0.778. No interaction.
In HER2-amplified (n=1504): beta=0.0170, p=0.939. REFUTED.
In HER2-non-amplified (n=48496): beta=-0.0455, p=0.234. Null as expected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even with progressively narrower restrictions (left-sided, then KRAS wt, then NRAS wt, then BRAF wt, then MSS), treatment_cetuximab does NOT improve pfs_months in this cohort.
Treatment_encorafenib does NOT improve pfs_months in BRAF V600E patients (braf_v600e=1), even when further restricted to KRAS wild-type, in this cohort.
Treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT improve pfs_months in MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) in this cohort.
Treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib does NOT improve pfs_months in HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified=1) in this cohort.

Treatment_regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated almost entirely in patients who are simultaneously KRAS-wild-type (kras_mutation=0), BRAF-V600E-wild-type (braf_v600e=0), left-sided primary (right_sided_primary=0), and have low pre-treatment CEA (cea_ng_ml < cohort median ≈ 4.44 ng/mL). In that subgroup the effect is dramatic (~+5 months PFS). In the COMPLEMENT (any unfavorable value: KRAS-mutant OR BRAF-V600E OR right-sided OR high CEA), the regorafenib effect on PFS is essentially zero.

Adding NRAS wild-type (nras_mutation=0) to the regorafenib responsive subgroup definition does not materially change the magnitude of the regorafenib benefit, because NRAS mutation is rare (~3%) in the cohort.
